title: pinus nigra subsp. dalmatica
intro: Pinus nigra subsp. dalmatica, also known as Dalmatian stone pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its seeds and resin, which have been used to treat various health issues. The seeds of the Dalmatian stone pine have been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health, while the resin has been used to treat coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the Dalmatian stone pine include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
